hypothesis: The best model so far uses tweedie_variance_power=1.5 (WRMSSE=0.94488). Hypothesis: lower tweedie_variance_power to 1.25 to put less weight on large sales values and more evenly distribute error across the distribution. WRMSSE weights high-revenue items more, so a lower variance power may align better with the metric by not over-penalizing predictions on low-volume items. All other settings match best run (exp_0033): num_leaves=256, max_depth=8, learning_rate=0.04, n_estimators=800, early_stopping_rounds=50.
